heading: False self-representation as registered entity member prohibited
label: 6h False self-representation as registered entity member prohibited
text: It shall be unlawful for any person falsely to represent such person to be a member of a registered entity or the representative or agent of such member, or to be a registrant under this chapter or the representative or agent of any registrant, in soliciting or handling any order or contract for the purchase or sale of any commodity in interstate commerce or for future delivery, or falsely to represent in connection with the handling of any such order or contract that the same is to be or has been executed on, or by or through a member of, any registered entity.
notes: Amendments2000— substituted “registered entity” for “contract market” in two places.Pub. L. 106–5541983— struck out provisions formerly designated as par. (1) relating to conduct of offices or places of business anywhere in the United States or its territories that were used for dealing in commodities for future delivery unless such dealings were executed or consummated by or through a member of a contract market, which provisions were transferred to , and broadened remaining provisions, formerly designated as par. (2), to prohibit false representations that a person is registered with the Commission in any capacity, and not only as a futures commission merchant, as previously provided.Pub. L. 97–444section 6(a) of this title
Effective Date of 1983 AmendmentAmendment by effective , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 97–444section 239 of Pub. L. 97–444section 2 of this titleJan. 11, 1983
Effective DateFor effective date of section, see section 13 of act , set out as an Effective Date of 1936 Amendment note under .June 15, 1936section 1 of this title
